author: Augustine of Hippo
book: A Treatise on Nature and Grace.

classification.cautionReason: No major mainstream heresy warning is attached by the preview classifier.

lineage: A Treatise on Nature and Grace.
A Man’s Sin is His Own, But He Needs Grace for His Cure.
title: A Man’s Sin is His Own, But He Needs Grace for His Cure.

verses.book: A Treatise on Nature and Grace.
verses.text: Chapter 34 [XXX.]—A Man’s Sin is His Own, But He Needs Grace for His Cure.

verses.book: A Treatise on Nature and Grace.
verses.text: Well, but what does he mean when he says: “Then again, how can one be subjected to God for the guilt of that sin, which he knows is not his own?

verses.book: A Treatise on Nature and Grace.
verses.text: For,” says he, “his own it is not, if it is necessary.

verses.book: A Treatise on Nature and Grace.
verses.text: Or, if it is his own, it is voluntary: and if it is voluntary, it can be avoided.”

verses.book: A Treatise on Nature and Grace.
verses.text: We reply: It is unquestionably his own.

verses.book: A Treatise on Nature and Grace.
verses.text: But the fault by which sin is committed is not yet in every respect healed, and the fact of its becoming permanently fixed in us arises from our not rightly using the healing virtue; and so out of this faulty condition the man who is now growing strong in depravity commits many sins, either through infirmity or blindness.

verses.book: A Treatise on Nature and Grace.
verses.text: Prayer must therefore be made for him, that he may be healed, and that he may thenceforward attain to a life of uninterrupted soundness of health; nor must pride be indulged in, as if any man were healed by the self-same power whereby he became corrupted.
